files.reason: Chaos/torture simulation with timing-sensitive behavior; useful for resilience, but flaky/long for normal CI.
files.suggested_action: Move to scheduled nightly chaos pipeline with explicit timeout budget and stability baseline.

files.reason: Concurrency and cache timing assertions are valuable but can flake under variable runtime scheduling.
files.suggested_action: Keep as non-blocking reliability suite; enforce retries or isolate with deterministic fakes.

files.reason: Extreme reconnect/replay/write-storm torture test; high runtime and instability risk.
files.suggested_action: Keep out of default CI; run in dedicated soak/torture stage.

files.reason: Broad matrix chaos integration with many scenarios; likely slow and noisy for fast feedback loops.
files.suggested_action: Run as periodic resilience certification, not per-commit gate.

files.reason: Recursive full-suite invocation from inside pytest can dramatically inflate runtime and duplicate coverage.
files.suggested_action: Disable in standard CI; run only as explicit pipeline job when requested.

files.reason: End-to-end simulation harness is useful but likely slow and timing-sensitive.
files.suggested_action: Shift to nightly/system-stage runs with strict wall-clock limits.

files.reason: Large harness-style security verification can overlap with torture/integration checks and run long.
files.suggested_action: Extract critical assertions into focused tests; keep bulk harness for scheduled audits.

files.reason: Stability-lock style checks are often timing dependent and environment sensitive.
files.suggested_action: Retain as review-only guard; avoid blocking fast CI.
